pubmed_links: [Yamada et al. (2012) Splicing variants of PLEKHA5 during brain development](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22037487/);[Jamain et al. (2014) Common and rare variant analysis in early-onset bipolar disorder vulnerability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25111785/)
summary: PLEKHA5 encodes a pleckstrin homology domain-containing protein with a brain-specific long isoform that participates in phosphoinositide signaling during neural development; variants in PLEKHA5 showed nominal association with early-onset bipolar disorder in a genome-wide study, implicating the phosphoinositide pathway in mood disorder susceptibility, though replication is needed. (novel candidate)
